name: Financial Services UX Patterns
description: User experience design for banking, fintech, and financial applications

pci_dss.name: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
pci_dss.scope: Apps that handle credit card data
pci_dss.ux_impact: Use Stripe, PayPal for card handling (don't build own)

regulations.gdpr_privacy: Right to access and delete financial data

practices: Encryption at rest and in transit

trust.description: Users must trust app with their money

clarity.description: Financial info must be crystal clear

practices: Show recipient info before sending money
Allow undo (if possible, e.g., pending transfers)

ux_patterns.transfers: Saved recipients, confirm before send, show delivery time

ux_patterns.send_money: Recipient autocomplete, amount large and clear, add note

ux_patterns.portfolio_view: Total value, gain/loss ($ and %), holdings breakdown
ux_patterns.stock_detail: Real-time price chart, buy/sell buttons, news feed

ux_patterns.spending_breakdown: Pie chart by category, compare to budget
ux_patterns.budget_setting: Suggest based on history, allow customization

ux_patterns.loan_application: Progress indicator, clear requirements, real-time approval
ux_patterns.rate_display: APR prominent, total cost shown, payment schedule
ux_patterns.disclosures: Terms and conditions clear, not buried

authentication.best_practice: Offer biometric for convenience, require 2FA for sensitive actions
authentication.session: Timeout after 5-10 min inactivity, require re-auth for sensitive actions

steps.1_amount: Large, clear input. Show balance. Suggest amounts (loan payoff, split bill).
steps.2_recipient: Autocomplete saved recipients. Show profile pic. Verify account/routing number.
steps.3_details: Add memo/note (optional). Select account (if multiple). Choose speed (instant fee vs free 1-3 days).
steps.4_review: Summary: To, From, Amount, Fee, Total, Delivery time. Edit option.
steps.5_confirm: 2FA or biometric. Confirmation message with transaction ID.
steps.6_receipt: Email/push notification. View in transaction history. Undo option (if pending).

fees_pricing.breakdown: Separate amount, fee, total. Explain each fee.
fees_pricing.comparison: If offering tiers, clear comparison table

data_visualization.spending: Pie chart by category, bar chart by month
data_visualization.portfolio: Line chart for performance over time, current value prominent
data_visualization.budget: Progress bars, green (under budget) / red (over budget)
data_visualization.best_practice: Show data, don't hide it in menus

error_states.insufficient_funds: Clear message: 'Not enough balance. Current: $50, Needed: $75.' Suggest add funds or lower amount.
error_states.invalid_account: 'Account number invalid. Please check and try again.' (Don't just say 'Error').
error_states.fraud_alert: 'Unusual activity detected. Confirm this is you.' Escalate to support if needed.
error_states.network_error: 'Connection lost. Your transaction did not go through. Try again.' (Don't charge if failed).

multi_factor_auth.ux: Offer multiple options, default to most secure, remember device (checkbox)

fraud_detection.confirm: 'Did you make this $500 purchase at X?' Yes/No buttons
fraud_detection.freeze: Quick freeze card option in app

password_reset.security: Send code to email + SMS, or security questions
password_reset.ux: Clear process, show expected wait time, don't lock account after failed attempts (provide recovery)

best_practices: Use 2FA for login and sensitive actions
Clear error messages with recovery steps
Encrypt all data (at rest and in transit)
Explain financial terms in plain language
Customer support easy to reach (in-app chat)
Test with diverse income levels and financial literacy
anti_patterns: No transaction confirmation for large amounts
Allowing transactions with insufficient funds (overdraft without warning)
No way to freeze card in app

regulations_compliance.gdpr: Right to access, delete, export data
regulations_compliance.pci_dss: Secure card data handling (use Stripe, not custom)
regulations_compliance.kyc_aml: Verify identity for accounts > certain amount
regulations_compliance.cfpb: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (US) - fair lending, clear disclosures

resources: Banking UX Design (Chase, Capital One case studies)
